timeline.event: The First Dawn: The gods awaken and create the world of Eldara.
The Shaping: Gods sculpt the lands, seas, and mountains.
The First Life: Creation of the first sentient beings, the Eldari.
The Great Forging: The Eldari create the first magical artifacts.
The Rise of the Drakonar: Dragon-like creatures become prominent.
The Coming of the Kael'thys: A race of elven people settle in Eldara.
The Sundering: The continent of Elyria splits into two smaller landmasses.
The Rise of the Dwarves: They emerge from their underground cities.
The Great War of the Ancients: Eldari and Drakonar clash over territory.
The Pact of the Three Races: Kael'thys, Eldari, and Dwarves form an alliance.
The Birth of the Human Race: Humans emerge in the wilds of Eldara.
The Founding of Arvandor: A great city built by the Kael'thys.
The First Human Kingdom: The kingdom of Valoria is established.
The Rise of the Orcs: They form their first tribal confederation.
The Great Migration: Humans and Kael'thys spread across Eldara.
The War of the Three Cities: Conflict between Arvandor, Valoria, and Thundertop.
The Treaty of the Silver Forest: Peace agreement ends the war.
The Awakening of the Giants: They emerge from their slumber.
The Founding of the Mage Guilds: Formalization of magical study and practice.
The Rise of the Dark Lord: A powerful mage turns to darkness and begins his conquest.

factions.name: Eldari
factions.description: A race of ancient beings with a deep connection to magic.

factions.name: Drakonar
factions.description: Dragon-like creatures who rule the skies and mountains.

factions.name: Kael'thys
factions.description: Elven people known for their grace and wisdom.

factions.name: Dwarves
factions.description: A hardy race of miners and craftsmen who live in underground cities.

factions.name: Humans
factions.description: Versatile and adaptable, they form numerous kingdoms across Eldara.

factions.name: Orcs
factions.description: Tribal warriors who live in the wild regions of Eldara.

factions.name: Giants
factions.description: Massive beings with a deep connection to nature and the elements.

factions.name: Halflings
factions.description: Small, cheerful folk known for their farming and hospitality.

factions.name: Goblins
factions.description: Cunning and resourceful, they often live in subterranean warrens.

factions.name: Trolls
factions.description: Large, brute-like creatures with regenerative abilities.

factions.name: Merfolk
factions.description: Aquatic humanoids who dwell in the seas of Eldara.

factions.name: Krakens
factions.description: Giant sea monsters that terrorize coastal regions.

places.name: Arvandor
places.description: A grand city of the Kael'thys, known for its beautiful architecture and magical gardens.

places.name: Valoria
places.description: The first human kingdom, a bustling center of trade and culture.

places.name: Thundertop
places.description: A fortress city built on a mountain, home to the Mage Guilds.

places.name: Elyria
places.description: The largest landmass in Eldara, split into two parts by The Sundering.

places.name: Silver Forest
places.description: An ancient forest that serves as a neutral ground for peace negotiations.

places.name: Mount Drakon
places.description: A towering peak where the Drakonar make their lairs.

places.name: Iron Hills
places.description: Rich in minerals, these hills are home to the Dwarves.

places.name: Ember Island
places.description: A volcanic island where fire elementals are said to dwell.

places.name: Azure Sea
places.description: A vast ocean that separates the continents of Elyria and Arvandor.

places.name: Mistwood
places.description: An enchanted forest where time flows differently.

places.name: Sunhold
places.description: A coastal city known for its sunny climate and vibrant marketplaces.

places.name: Moonhaven
places.description: A tranquil city built in a crescent moon shape, home to the Eldari.

places.name: Cragstone Keep
places.description: A formidable fortress on the edge of the Iron Hills.

places.name: Whispering Plains
places.description: Vast open plains where the wind carries ancient secrets.

places.name: Stonefang Pass
places.description: A narrow path through the mountains, often guarded by trolls.

places.name: Silverlake
places.description: A large, serene lake with a silver sheen on its surface.

places.name: Firewatch Tower
places.description: A tall tower that serves as an observatory and lookout point.

places.name: Shadow Vale
places.description: A dark, mysterious valley shrouded in perpetual twilight.

places.name: Starfall Caverns
places.description: An extensive network of caves where meteors are said to have fallen long ago.

places.name: Eclipse Island
places.description: A mystical island that experiences frequent lunar eclipses.

magic_rules: Magic is fueled by the life force of living beings and can be drawn from nature, animals, or even the caster's own vitality.
Spells require a verbal component, a somatic gesture, and a material focus to channel the magical energy.
Magical artifacts can amplify the power of spells and are often sought after by mages and adventurers.
Certain regions of Eldara have unique magical properties that affect spellcasting, such as the Silver Forest or Ember Island.
The study of magic is divided into schools: Elemental (fire, water, earth, air), Arcane (divination, enchantment, illusion), and Divine (healing, protection, blessing).
Magical creatures can be bonded with casters to enhance their abilities or serve as familiars.
The power of a spell is limited by the caster's knowledge, skill, and the strength of their magical source.
Rituals and ceremonies can increase the potency of magic, especially when performed in sacred locations.
hard_constraints: The gods created Eldara and are the ultimate source of all life and magic.
The First Dawn occurred 3000 years before the birth of humans.
Elyria was a single continent until The Sundering, which split it into two landmasses.
The Kael'thys, Eldari, and Dwarves formed an alliance known as the Pact of the Three Races in -400.
Arvandor is the first city built by the Kael'thys and remains their primary stronghold.
Valoria is the oldest human kingdom and serves as a central hub for trade and culture.
The Silver Forest is a neutral ground where peace treaties are often negotiated.
Mount Drakon is the lair of the most powerful Drakonar, and no other faction can claim it without a significant conflict.
The Iron Hills are rich in minerals and are home to the Dwarves' largest underground cities.
Ember Island is a volcanic island where fire elementals dwell, and it is considered sacred by many factions.
The Azure Sea separates Elyria from Arvandor and is a major trade route.
Mistwood is an enchanted forest where time flows differently, making it a place of mystery and ancient magic.
Sunhold is the largest coastal city in Eldara and is known for its sunny climate and bustling marketplaces.
Moonhaven is a tranquil city built by the Eldari and is shaped like a crescent moon.
Cragstone Keep is a formidable fortress that guards the entrance to the Iron Hills.
The Whispering Plains are vast open plains where ancient secrets can be heard in the wind.
Stonefang Pass is a narrow mountain pass often guarded by trolls, making it a strategic location for travel and trade.
Silverlake has a silver sheen on its surface due to magical properties of the surrounding land.
Firewatch Tower serves as an observatory and lookout point, providing a panoramic view of the region.
Shadow Vale is a dark, mysterious valley shrouded in perpetual twilight, known for its connection to shadow magic.
Starfall Caverns are an extensive network of caves where meteors are said to have fallen long ago, imbuing the area with unique magical properties.
Eclipse Island experiences frequent lunar eclipses and is considered a place of mystical significance.
